publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-08-08 18:42:35 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Letters and ledgers from various members of the Merritt family.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Letters from various members of the Merritt family, describing life along the Saint Lawrence River and in Saint Lawrence County and their pioneering travel west to Iowa, Wisconsin, Kansas, and Nebraska; much local color. Also several financial ledgers, account and day books, and some various publications.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Merritt family papers, #39J. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.p: Address: Pierrepont, St. Lawrence County, NY Sender: Emily, Ellen, Parker N. Merritt Letter #4

scopecontent.p: Address: Sudbury, Vermont Sender: Darwin H. Merritt Letter #5

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address: Pierrepont, St. Lawrence County, NY Sender: Parker N. Merritt Letter #6

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address: Liverpool, Onandaga County, NY Sender: Cyrus Burnap Letter #7 Letter seems to be a business transaction. Negative references are made to a Mr. Miller and Mr. Hoffman. Cyrus writes with concern about a money exchange, and the sale of logs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address: East Pierrepont, St. Lawrence County, NY Sender: Harrison Y. Willson Letter #8

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address: East Pierrepont, St. Lawrence County, NY Sender: H. H. Merritt Letter #10

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address: Burlington, VT. Sender: Noadiah Merritt Letter #13 Mr. Merritt addresses a grievance brought up in a previous letter from Mr. Porter. Accusations of property damage are brought up. Mr. Merritt closes by quoting several lines from the Bible: "Do unto others as ye would that they should do unto you." ; "Let him that is without sin cast the first stone." ; "Judge not lest ye be judged."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address: Albany, NY Sender: Anson Tolman Letter #14

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address: Normal School, Albany, NY Sender: Leonard Letter #3 Leonard writes William about money that he, Mr. Merritt, owes to "the division." The amount totals one dollar and ninety-five cents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sender: Henry. W. Merritt Letter #10 appears to be a letter written by a young child. H. H. Merritt writes on the back that it is Henry's first letter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address: Crary's Mills, St. Lawrence County, NY Letter #12

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter #14 What seems to be a poem

scopecontent.p: Address: Crary's Mills, St. Lawrence County, NY Sender: E. Merritt Letter #4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sender: Jessie Fremont, Jennie Merritt Letter #16 Letter discussing the New York's prohibition laws and the states seeming support of a "Whiskey President".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Address: Crary's Mills, St. Lawrence County, NY Letter #19 A Carte Postale sent from Paris, France. The front translates: "This side is reserved exclusively for the address"

did.unittitle: Speech of Honorable D. Wilmot, of Pennsylvania, in the House of Representatives discussing slavery in the territories
Article discussing the Northwestern Sanitary Fair (2 pages)
Entire page from a newspaper. Various printed advertisements as well as a lengthy article on the report of the New York State Fisheries Commissioners.
Envelope advertises Art Metal Light Weight Safes and a promise of burglary insurance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publisher: Art Metal Construction Co. Jamestown, NY

did.unittitle: Postcard to be filled out and sent in to receive free burglary insurance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publisher: Art Metal Construction Co. Jamestown, NY

did.unittitle: Direction booklet for the Yale Combination Lock

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publisher: The Yale & Towne Mfg. Co.

did.unittitle: Darwin H. Merritt Day Book: record of financial transactions
Record of financial transactions kept by Darwin H. Merritt
Darwin H. Merritt Account Book: record of financial transactions
Record of financial transactions kept by D. H. Merritt, Pierrepont, NY
Record of accounts kept by Darwin H. Merritt
Record of financial transactions (Very fragile: binding broken)
